steps.title: Identify the key evidence
steps.content: The crucial finding connects two things: the clam garden practice described in songs and actual growth in clam size and abundance despite increased harvesting pressure. This correspondence demonstrates the method was effective.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D directly states what the finding demonstrates: “effective methods for the cultivation of sources of sustenance are among the ecological knowledge preserved in Indigenous songs.” The clam gardens are methods for cultivating a food source preserved in songs, and the finding confirms they were effective.

The other three either claim an unsupported “defining characteristic” (A), focus on motivation rather than the evidence (B), or assert a species change contradicted by the growth data (C).

This matches choice D — D is correct.
